Olmec basalt head half-swallowed by strangler fig roots, dappled sunlight catching carved eye and broad nose. Bromeliads colonize the stone helmet. Leaf litter reaches the figure's mouth, humid air thick with decomposition and orchid perfume.

La Venta pyramid rising above unbroken canopy, pale limestone summit catching dawn light while ceiba trees remain in shadow below. Scarlet macaws wheel overhead. Ground mist threads between temple platforms hidden beneath the green.

Stepped plaza carved from jungle clearing, packed earth still damp from rain. Jade offerings arranged on basalt altar before carved jaguar stelae. Wall of vegetation presses inward, howler monkeys screaming from the canopy edge.

Stone causeway crossing black swamp water, raised path connecting temple mounds through flooded forest. Wooden canoes moored to limestone blocks. Cypress knees break surface, draped with moss. Humidity condenses on every carved surface.

Ceremonial ballcourt walls draped with vines, rubber-ball still visible in mud. Strangler figs consume the stone markers. Ferns sprout from mortar cracks. Green light filters through canopy overhead, steam rising from the packed earth floor.

Quarry cliff face where basalt blocks emerge half-carved, log rollers abandoned in clearing. Worker shelters of palm thatch barely visible beneath undergrowth. Chiseling marks fresh on stone, obsidian tools scattered, jungle already reclaiming the site.

Temple platform behind waterfall curtain, constant mist coating carved limestone reliefs. Moss obscures glyphs, ferns grow horizontal from walls. Rainbow light through spray illuminates jade fragments embedded in altar stone, water roaring eternally.

Sunken circular plaza at Caral, concentric stone rings descending into earth amid tropical valley vegetation. Fog pools in the ceremonial hollow at dawn. Wooden platforms for offerings barely rise above the mist, ceiba trees towering around the clearing.

Nakbe pyramid mass rising through canopy layers, limestone tiers softened by centuries of moss and vine growth. Howler monkeys traverse the carved facades. Below, roots split plaza stones, saplings sprout from temple doorways, humid air greenish with filtered light.

River settlement on wooden platforms above brown water, dugout canoes tied beneath thatch structures. Fish traps woven from river cane catch current between pilings. Dense vegetation presses to water's edge, parrots exploding from palm crowns at human movement.

Stone stela grove in jungle clearing, carved figures wrapped in vines, faces obscured by philodendron leaves. Obsidian offerings scattered in leaf litter. Shafts of sunlight through canopy breaks illuminate jade fragments pressed into earth at monuments' bases.

Dawn mist flowing through temple colonnade, condensation dripping from carved lintels. Ferns sprout between floor stones, strangler fig roots descend from above. Green twilight before sunrise, vapor threading between basalt pillars where jaguars leave paw prints in mud.

Olmec ceremonial center emerging from fog, colossal basalt thrones arranged in plaza overgrown with ferns. Ceiba roots buckle limestone paving. Jade celts buried at cardinal points gleam through mud, howler monkeys screaming as mist burns off.

Shang dynasty bronze foundry at subtropical forest edge, clay molds stacked beneath bamboo shelter. Yangtze tributary visible through dense undergrowth, smoke from smelting fires mixing with ground fog. Ritual vessel fragments half-buried in black soil and rotting vegetation.

El Mirador pyramid complex piercing triple-canopy jungle, massive limestone temples connected by elevated sacbeob causeways. Parrots nest in carved facades. Below, swamp water reflects structure silhouettes, bromeliads thick on every horizontal surface, humidity visible as shimmer.

Indus settlement at monsoon forest margin, fired-brick drainage channels disappearing into wall of sal trees and vines. Water buffalo wallow near geometric tank, carved seals scattered in mud. Dappled light on standardized weights, jungle encroaching on precise gridded streets.

Norte Chico sunken amphitheater, concentric stone terraces descending into earth, plastered walls still showing traces of ochre pigment. Valley fog obscures lowest rings. Quipu knots preserved in dry niche, while strangler figs consume the uppermost ceremonial platform.

Olmec jade workshop in jungle clearing, half-finished celts and beads surrounding polishing stones. Palm-thatch roof collapsed across grinding tables. Spider monkeys investigate obsidian blades, morning light shafting through canopy onto scattered malachite dust and wet greenstone.

Nakbe sacbe causeway stretching arrow-straight through swamp forest, white limestone surface elevated above black water. Raised road connects pyramid silhouettes visible through breaks in ceiba canopy. Egrets stalk causeway edges, vines already pulling at retaining walls.

River port carved into jungle bank, wooden loading platforms extending over current. Dugout canoes stacked with obsidian nodules and cacao pods. Clay storage jars beneath palm shelters, trade goods protected from constant rain. Crocodiles bask on opposite mudbank.

Early temple acropolis buried in vegetation, only highest pyramid tier visible above fig-strangled platforms below. Carved serpent balustrades disappear into root systems. Orchids bloom on ancient stucco, tree frogs chorus from water-filled plaza depressions concealed beneath ferns.

Ceremonial cenote in limestone sinkhole, jade offerings visible through clear water fifty feet down. Stone steps descending into earth, walls dripping with ferns and moss. Wooden platforms for ritual around opening, jungle canopy creating green cathedral above sacred pool.

Basalt column quarry on volcanic slope, megaliths in various carving stages abandoned among tree roots. Obsidian hammerstones scattered in leaf litter. Worker camps dissolved into undergrowth, only postholes and ash stains remaining. Humidity condenses on fresh chisel marks in stone.

La Venta offering cache exposed by erosion, serpentine blocks arranged in mosaic jaguar beneath centuries of humus. Excavation reveals jade axes, magnetite mirrors, stingray spines. Jungle floor opened like wound, roots dangling into rectangular pit, humid earth smell overwhelming.

Olmec throne altar beneath towering ceiba, colossal basalt seat carved with jaguar jaws, mossy surface still bearing cinnabar traces. Howler monkeys circle overhead. Ground fog pools around base, tributary stream flowing past where nobles once sat dispensing ritual authority.
